purpose: Let the user plan an event end-to-end: pick a venue, manage guests, tick off prep tasks, and run the day-of itinerary.
primaryUserGoal: Organize an event from idea to execution without losing track of moving pieces.
whenHermesShouldChoose: User asks to plan a party, dinner, birthday, wedding, offsite, or similar gathering that needs a venue + guests + checklist.
supports: Tabs: Venue (card-grid with venue images), Guests (grouped-list), Checklist, Itinerary (timeline), Notes
Guest grouping by RSVP or dietary restriction
Day-of timeline with arrival, toasts, dinner, cake

populationInstructions.summary: Create a tabs section with 5 panes: Venue (card-grid with venue images), Guests (grouped-list with RSVP tone), Checklist, Itinerary (timeline for event day), Notes.
steps: Parse event type, date/time, headcount, budget.
Venue tab: card-grid of 2-4 venue options, each with image.query ('<venue name>' or '<venue type> <city>'), price/capacity, and 2-3 bullets.
Guests tab: grouped-list by RSVP status (Yes, No, Pending). Include dietary notes.
Checklist tab: prep tasks with a few prerequisites.
Itinerary tab: timeline of the event day with times.
Notes tab: free-form for contacts, song requests, etc.
guardrails: Never include guests' personal contact info in free text — only names + RSVP.
Don't invent venue availability — mark as 'check with venue'.
Keep the Checklist focused on prep; day-of details belong in Itinerary.

patchPrefer: Use upsert_cards to add/refresh venue options
Use upsert_groups to update RSVP status
Use set_active_tab as the user shifts focus
replaceTriggers: User pivots to a completely different event (different date + theme)
